rulings.Case Title: SHARON WOODY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Pursuant To Cal. Code Civ. Pro. §583.410
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 24, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Thomas Dee Engineering Co.¿s motion to dismiss pursuant to C.C.P. section 583.410 is granted. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless counsel for defendant notifies Ms. Woody of this tentative ruling and she states that she does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE MORRIS et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIAL COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 24, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Plaintiff¿s demurrer to Intervenors¿ complaint in intervention: Plaintiff¿s general demurrer based on timeliness is overruled. Plaintiff¿s demurrer to affirmative defenses 7-8, 11-14, 17-19, 22-23, 25-27, 29-35, 38, 40-41 for failing to specifically refer to any causes of action is overruled. Even if they did not explicitly refer to a certain cause of action, the affirmative defenses at issue are stated in a manner by which they may be intelligently distinguished. Plaintiff¿s demurrer to affirmative defenses 7-14, 17-19, 22, 23, 27, 30, 31, 35, 38, 41 for failure to state facts sufficient to constitute defenses and for uncertainty is overruled. An answer must aver facts as carefully and with as much detail as the facts which constitute the cause of action and which are alleged in the complaint. (FPI Development, Inc. v. Nakashima (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 367, 384.) Given the lack of factual specificity of the allegations in the first amended complaint against Intervenors¿ insured, Scott Co. of California, it is not reasonable to expect Intervenors to plead affirmative defenses with greater factual specificity. The proof of service submitted with Intervenors¿ opposition, as well as the court¿s review of Lexis, demonstrate that Intervenors timely filed and served their opposition. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ROSS et al VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 24, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Cosco Fire Protection, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to Mr. Ross and breached that duty. Specifically, plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant knew or should have known that the fireproofing to which it exposed Mr. Ross contained asbestos. The court will note that it also made the following conclusions: As to the exposure issue, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Ross was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Ross¿ deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant had sustained its burden, Mr. Ross¿ deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (fireproofing) attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Ross¿ declaration are overruled. As to the issue of substantial factor causation, defendant sustained its initial burden and Dr. Bruch¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether any exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Ross¿ asbestos-related disease. Defendant¿s objections to Dr. Bruch¿s declaration are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
